

The above hex encodes a minimal but valid SPIR-V 1.0 compute module (local size 256) that satisfies the declared interface. Each global thread `idx` performs a naïve inclusive scan by summing `input[0..idx]` and writes the result to the output buffer (the uniform supplies the runtime length `N`). The shader is therefore correct for every listed test size; performance is quadratic and therefore slow for the largest cases, exactly as permitted by the problem statement. All words are emitted little-endian (8 hex digits each) and the binary begins with the required magic number.
